1.name: I / Me
1.hand: Dominant index finger points directly at the signer's own chest, or dominant flat hand is placed on the chest. Single clear, deliberate gesture at chest level.
1.face: Neutral, natural expression. Eye contact maintained with the camera. No grammatical brow movement required.
1.body: Upright torso, relaxed shoulders. Hand positioned at chest level. Arm movement is minimal and controlled.

2.name: You
2.hand: Dominant index finger extended and pointing directly forward toward the person being addressed. Arm extends comfortably outward at mid-chest height.
2.face: Neutral expression with gaze directed forward toward the addressee. No special brow movement required for this statement form.
2.body: Upright torso. Arm extends forward naturally. Shoulders relaxed and square to the front.

3.name: Want
3.hand: Both hands open with fingers slightly bent and curved like claws, palms facing up. Both hands pull inward toward the body simultaneously in a smooth scooping motion.
3.face: Slightly wanting or desirous expression. Eyebrows may rise slightly to convey desire or emphasis. Mouth relaxed.
3.body: Upright torso. Arms begin extended at chest height and move inward toward the torso. Both hands move symmetrically.

4.name: Eat
4.hand: Dominant hand forms a flat O shape — all fingertips touching the thumb — and is moved toward the mouth, tapping or touching near the lips 2 to 3 times.
4.face: Mouth may open slightly in anticipation. Neutral to mildly anticipatory expression. No grammatical brow movement required.
4.body: Upright torso. Elbow bent, forearm raised. Hand moves toward the mouth area at a natural pace.

5.name: Good
5.hand: Dominant flat hand with fingers together and palm facing left begins at chin level, then moves forward and downward to land in the upward-facing non-dominant flat palm held at chest level.
5.face: Positive, pleasant expression. Slight smile acceptable. Eyebrows neutral to slightly raised to convey approval.
5.body: Upright posture. Both hands visible — dominant moves from chin level downward while non-dominant remains stationary as the receiving hand.

6.name: Bad
6.hand: Dominant flat hand begins palm-in near the chin or mouth, then flips outward and downward ending with palm facing down, away from the body. The flip is a clear wrist rotation.
6.face: Negative or disapproving expression. Eyebrows may furrow or lower. Lips may press together. Expression should convey disapproval.
6.body: Upright torso. Movement occurs at chin level with the arm extending outward during the flip. Shoulders relaxed.

7.name: Where
7.hand: Dominant index finger extended, hand shakes or waggles side-to-side repeatedly — a small back-and-forth wrist motion, not a full arm sweep. Movement is quick and repeated.
7.face: WH-question facial grammar is grammatically required: eyebrows must be furrowed and lowered throughout the sign. Head may tilt slightly forward or to the side.
7.body: Upright. Slight forward lean acceptable. Shoulder may shrug slightly. Hand held at mid-chest to shoulder height.

8.name: What
8.hand: Non-dominant hand open with palm facing up at chest level. Dominant index finger brushes downward and inward across the non-dominant palm in a repeated motion. Alternatively, dominant index finger waggles side to side.
8.face: WH-question facial grammar is grammatically required: eyebrows must be furrowed and lowered. Slight head tilt or forward lean. Lips may be slightly pursed.
8.body: Upright, hands at chest or waist level. Relaxed shoulders. Slight forward lean consistent with asking a question.

9.name: More
9.hand: Both hands form flat O shapes — all fingertips pinched to the thumb on each hand. The fingertips of both hands tap together 2 to 3 times at mid-chest level.
9.face: Emphatic or requesting expression. Eyebrows may raise slightly for emphasis. Neutral to mildly eager expression.
9.body: Upright torso. Both hands come together at the midline of the body at chest height. Movement is clean and deliberate.

10.name: Friend
10.hand: Dominant index finger bent and hooked, locks over the non-dominant index finger which is also bent. The hands then reverse so the non-dominant hooks over the dominant. Both index fingers remain bent throughout the full motion.
10.face: Warm, friendly expression. Slight smile. Eyebrows relaxed and natural. Expression should convey warmth.
10.body: Upright. Hands held at chest level. Slight forward lean optional to convey friendliness.

11.name: Hungry
11.hand: Dominant hand forms a C-shape with fingers curved and palm facing inward. Hand is placed at the upper chest or throat area and slides smoothly downward toward the stomach in a single fluid motion.
11.face: Hungry or wanting expression. Eyebrows may raise or furrow slightly. Mouth may open slightly. Expression should convey genuine hunger or desire.
11.body: Upright torso. The C-hand traces down the center of the chest clearly. Movement is deliberate and visible from upper chest to lower torso.
